10: The code sets up a tool-calling agent using the LangChain library and the Google
11: Gemini model. It defines a search_information tool that simulates providing factual
12: answers to specific queries. The tool has predefined responses for "weather in
13: london," "capital of france," and "population of earth," and a default response for
14: other queries. A ChatGoogleGenerativeAI model is initialized, ensuring it has
15: tool-calling capabilities. A ChatPromptTemplate is created to guide the agent's
16: interaction. The create_tool_calling_agent function is used to combine the language
17: model, tools, and prompt into an agent. An AgentExecutor is then set up to manage
18: the agent's execution and tool invocation. The run_agent_with_tool asynchronous
19: function is defined to invoke the agent with a given query and print the result. The
20: main asynchronous function prepares multiple queries to be run concurrently. These
21: queries are designed to test both the specific and default responses of the
22: search_information tool. Finally, the asyncio.run(main()) call executes all the agent
23: tasks. The code includes checks for successful LLM initialization before proceeding
26: This code provides a practical example of how to implement function calling (Tools)
27: within the CrewAI framework. It sets up a simple scenario where an agent is equipped
28: with a tool to look up information. The example specifically demonstrates fetching a
29: simulated stock price using this agent and tool.
